name: Houthis (Yemen)
summary: Day 91 (Saturday May 23). **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 5 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS; NO KINETIC ATTACK ON HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL CONFIRMED MAY 20-23 IN MAJOR MARITIME TRACKING OUTLETS. LLOYD'S JWC DAY 5 OF 7 WITH NO EASTERN MED LISTING CHANGE — INSURANCE MARKET NOT BUYING THE THREAT.** Five days after Saree announcement, no major maritime tracking outlet (Maritime Executive, Ship & Bunker, Seatrade Maritime, The Loadstar, Supply Chain Brain) has reported a confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel. Lloyd's Joint War Committee Listed Areas update JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026) remains current published listing — no Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa addition through May 23. **The cleanest external test of credibility — the insurance market — has not endorsed the Houthi threat.** Atlantic Council framing holds to Day 5: 'Houthis stay technically within bounds of agreement with Washington while issuing direct threats against Israeli targets.' The Loadstar May 22-23 introduces a new tangent — Houthis may target 'Egypt's Red Sea ports' — but remains rhetoric only without operational confirmation. **Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med trigger Day 5 of 7 (by 2026-05-26) remains `watching`; Haifa-kinetic-attack trigger Day 5 of 14 (by 2026-06-02) remains `watching`.** **The Houthi proxy network continues to demonstrate graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — the same Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric coexists with deal architecture. Times of Israel + Haaretz May 22-23 carry no Haifa-attack reporting; AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 (Argus, Lloyd's List wire); UKMTO has not issued fresh advisory. PRIOR (May 22): **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 4 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS, NO CONFIRMED KINETIC ATTACK ON HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL, LLOYD'S JWC HAS NOT ADDED EASTERN MED.** Three days after the May 19 announcement (Houthi spokesman Saree, Communications Center for Red Sea Traffic), no confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel has surfaced in major maritime tracking outlets (Maritime Executive, Ship & Bunker, Seatrade Maritime, Supply Chain Brain — all running the May 17-19 announcement story; no kinetic follow-through reported May 20-22). **Lloyd's Joint War Committee Listed Areas update JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026)** added Bahrain/Djibouti/Kuwait/Oman/Qatar to the Persian Gulf coverage area — but NO subsequent update has added Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa (Insurance Journal, LMA Joint War Committee, Lloyd's List). **The 'Lloyd's JWC adds Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa to high-risk listing within 7 days (by 2026-05-26)' trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 7** — the cleanest external test of credibility still uncrossed. The 'confirmed Houthi kinetic attack on Haifa-bound shipping within 14 days' trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 14. **Atlantic Council framing — 'Houthis stay technically within bounds of agreement with Washington while issuing direct threats against Israeli targets' — is validated to Day 4**: rhetoric WITHOUT operational follow-through is the dominant pattern, not capability-test failure. **Israeli media confirms negative space**: Times of Israel May 22 liveblog and Haaretz carry no Haifa-attack incident reporting for May 20-22; insurance market AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels (Argus, Lloyd's List). **The Houthi proxy network is demonstrating graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric can coexist with deal architecture. PRIOR (May 19): **A6 HOUTHIS ANNOUNCED 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' OF ISRAEL'S HAIFA PORT — CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NO ACTION; REVERSES MONDAY'S 'COORDINATED QUIETISM' READING.** Spokesman Yahya Saree said in a televised statement that 'Haifa has, from the moment of this statement's release, become one of our targets' and warned all companies with ships present in or heading to Haifa that the port had been 'included in the list of targets' (Reuters wire primary via Yahoo/gCaptain/Ship&Bunker; Maritime Executive; Anadolu Agency). The Houthi Communications Center for Red Sea Traffic added that a 'long-distance naval blockade' would apply, with all vessels 'prohibited' from loading or unloading at Israel's largest seaport. **The announcement IS rhetorically calibrated**: (i) targets Israel directly, not US/UK shipping; (ii) Haifa is on the Israeli MEDITERRANEAN coast, not Red Sea — 'long-distance naval blockade' of a Mediterranean port from Yemen is more rhetorical than operational; (iii) the announcement is justified by Gaza, NOT by Iran-US kinetic preparations — preserving the Houthis' option to claim they haven't entered the Iran-US theater. **The Maritime-Executive headline uses scare quotes around 'blockade' precisely because it has NOT been operationalized through confirmed attacks on shipping at Haifa.** Saree cited the Eilat-port blockade as precedent (REPORTED — Houthi claim that Eilat 'has since ceased operations'; independent maritime confirmation pending). **B4 The Eilat-blockade precedent is structurally weak**: Eilat is Red Sea-adjacent to Yemen's launch zones; Haifa is on the Mediterranean, far from Houthi missile/drone reach. The credibility-projection is rhetorical, not operational. **Atlantic Council / Stimson framing**: 'targeted provocations highlight the fundamental problem with partial regional ceasefires — they merely redirect rather than resolve hostilities.' The 'coordinated quietism' framing (Monday May 18 Day 7 UNTRIPPED) was a graduated-rhetoric pattern, NOT a structural restraint. **Iran's proxy network can pivot from non-attack (last 7 days) to verbal-blockade (today) within 48 hours.** **NEW trigger watch**: confirmed missile/drone aimed at Haifa-bound shipping within 7 days = CONFIRMED ACTION upgrade; silence by Day 7 = 'rhetoric-only' tag holds. Lloyds JWC EastMed AWRP movement will be the cleanest operational test. PRIOR (May 18): **A4 RED SEA NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW DAY 7 OF 7 CLOSES UNTRIPPED** — no second incident confirmed since May 11 Hodeidah; UKMTO recent-incidents page shows no new advisory through Monday morning IST; MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement. May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event,' not start of new pattern. **Houthi non-action during a publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase** (NYT preparation May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu call May 17, Saudi drone interception May 17, UAE Barakah drone strike May 17) **is the cleanest behavioural divergence from the March-April pattern** when Houthi attacks compounded with US escalation rhetoric. Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification of Trump posts — coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise. Investor read: Iran signalling escalation control, not escalation. **PRIOR (May 17): B4 HOUTHIS RED SEA — NO CLAIMED OR UNCLAIMED MARITIME INCIDENT THROUGH MAY 17 MORNING IST. Negative-space holds Day 6 of 7-day window from May 11 Hodeidah.** No fresh UKMTO advisory through May 17 morning IST. The May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident still stands as a single event — no second Red Sea attack within the 7-day window opened. Project Freedom pause Day 12; CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61. **The Houthi non-action persists through the weekend during which Trump-Iran is publicly hardening — Houthi quiet during a US kinetic-preparation phase is structurally significant intelligence, not a coincidence.** If no incident occurs by ~May 18 (Day 7), the May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of a new pattern. Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92% from Feb-Mar peak per JPost + CNN intel — Iran-aligned proxy network operating well below historic kinetic ceiling. **PRIOR (May 14):** Day 82. A8 NEGATIVE-SPACE: Day 3 of 7 since May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed explosion — UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page shows NO second incident in southern Red Sea / Hodeidah corridor through May 13-14 morning** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO operational primary). If no second incident occurs by ~May 18 (Day 7), the May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of a new pattern. CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61 (no fresh redirect actions). The Houthi non-claim of May 11 remains the operative signal. Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92% from Feb-Mar peak per JPost + CNN intel — Iran-aligned proxy network operating well below historic kinetic ceiling. PRIOR (May 13): UKMTO reported multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km (80 mi) southwest of Hodeidah on Monday May 11; the ship continued voyage with no injuries, and the vessel was briefly 'not under command' (CONFIRMED ACTION — UKMTO advisory primary, Times of Israel corroborator). **Houthis did NOT claim responsibility — attribution UNCONFIRMED.** The non-claim is itself a signal: possibilities include (i) deniable Houthi action (Iran-aligned signal without attribution), (ii) Houthi action awaiting domestic framing, (iii) unrelated incident. This represents a potential break of the cycle's most stable non-event (80+ days of zero Red Sea attacks during Iran's multi-axis kinetic restart). **If a second Red Sea incident occurs within 7 days of May 11, the Houthi non-action read collapses; if not, this remains 'single incident, unclaimed.'** Until then: pattern undeclared, sample size 1. UN Security Council Resolution 2812 (2026) framework (monthly reporting through July 15) remains structurally intact pending attribution. Prior coordination-decay framework spanned Iran's May 4 kinetic restart, Project Freedom + pause cycle, May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks, May 8 US tanker strikes, May 8 Hezbollah Mt. Meron drone reach. Persistent VLCC/Frontline rate elevation remains HORMUZ-driven, NOT Bab el-Mandeb-driven.

active_triggers.condition: Houthis begin confirmed Red Sea attacks on NEUTRAL commercial shipping (verified by maritime trackers) — Day 47 attacks RESUMED but ONLY on belligerent-linked vessels (US/UK/Israeli). Neutral shipping
Houthis launch confirmed attack on Israel (missile/drone reaching Israeli territory)
Confirmed Houthi kinetic attack on Haifa-bound shipping within 14 days of May 19 'maritime blockade' announcement (by 2026-06-02)
active_triggers.note: Saree announcement May 19 of 'maritime blockade' on Haifa is CONFIRMED RHETORIC; the trigger tests whether the verbal blockade graduates to operational attack. Lloyds JWC EastMed AWRP movement is leading indicator. Confirmed kinetic = action-tag upgrade; silence Day 14 = 'rhetoric-only' tag holds. Mediterranean reach from Yemen is operationally weaker than Red Sea reach — favors silence outcome.

active_triggers.note: Operational test of how the shipping community is reading the Saree-Haifa announcement — if Lloyds raises EastMed AWRP, the credibility weight of Houthi reach to Mediterranean is being priced; if Lloyds does not move, the 'rhetoric-only' tag is confirmed by the insurance market.

recent_signals.headline: **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 4 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS, NO CONFIRMED KINETIC ATTACK; LLOYD'S JWC HAS NOT ADDED EASTERN MED.** Three days after May 19 announcement, no confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel has surfaced in major maritime tracking outlets (Maritime Executive, Ship & Bunker, Seatrade Maritime, Supply Chain Brain). **Lloyd's Joint War Committee Listed Areas update JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026)** added Bahrain/Djibouti/Kuwait/Oman/Qatar to Persian Gulf coverage — but NO subsequent update has added Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa. **Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 7** (by 2026-05-26); confirmed kinetic attack trigger `watching` Day 4 of 14. **Israeli media confirms negative space**: Times of Israel May 22 liveblog + Haaretz carry no Haifa-attack incident reporting May 20-22; insurance market AWRP ~1% holding from May 19. **Atlantic Council framing validated to Day 4**: 'Houthis stay technically within bounds of agreement with Washington while issuing direct threats against Israeli targets' — rhetoric WITHOUT operational follow-through is the dominant pattern. **The Houthi proxy network is demonstrating graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric can coexist with deal architecture. The simultaneity with the IRGC May 21 'global offensive' threat (CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NO ACTION) reinforces the pattern: rhetorical escalation along multiple axes, but the operational floor holds.
sources: Insurance Journal March 3 'JWLA-033 area expansion' (no subsequent change confirmed)
Lloyd's List wire May 17-22 (no Eastern Med listing change)
Maritime Executive (May 17 announcement, no May 20-22 update)
Ship & Bunker / Seatrade Maritime / Supply Chain Brain (no kinetic follow-through)
Times of Israel May 22 + Haaretz (negative-space confirmation)
recent_signals.verification: Houthi Haifa kinetic attack = CONFIRMED ABSENCE Day 4 of 14 (multi-source maritime tracking); Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med listing change = CONFIRMED ABSENCE Day 4 of 7 (Lloyd's List wire); AWRP ~1% = CONFIRMED operational data (Argus); Atlantic Council framing of restraint = INFERRED structural reading.

recent_signals.headline: **HOUTHIS ANNOUNCED 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' OF ISRAEL'S HAIFA PORT — CONFIRMED RHETORIC PIVOT, NO OPERATIONAL FOLLOW-THROUGH; REVERSES MONDAY'S 'COORDINATED QUIETISM' READING.** Spokesman Yahya Saree said in a televised statement: 'Haifa has, from the moment of this statement's release, become one of our targets' and warned all companies with ships present in or heading to Haifa that the port had been 'included in the list of targets.' The Houthi Communications Center for Red Sea Traffic added that a 'long-distance naval blockade' would apply, with all vessels 'prohibited' from loading or unloading at Israel's largest seaport. The decision was framed as response to Israeli military operations in Gaza, **NOT** as response to US-Iran kinetic preparations — a subtle but consequential framing choice. **The announcement IS calibrated**: (i) targets Israel directly, not US/UK shipping; (ii) Haifa is on the Israeli MEDITERRANEAN coast, not Red Sea — 'long-distance naval blockade' of a Mediterranean port from Yemen is more rhetorical than operational; (iii) the announcement is justified by Gaza, preserving Houthi option to claim they haven't entered the Iran-US theater. **Maritime Executive headline uses scare quotes around 'blockade' precisely because it has NOT been operationalized**: no attack on a vessel attempting to dock at Haifa has been independently confirmed. Saree cited the Eilat-port blockade as precedent and claimed Eilat 'has since ceased operations' (REPORTED Houthi claim; independent maritime confirmation pending — Eilat is Red Sea-adjacent, structurally different from Haifa's Mediterranean position). Atlantic Council / Stimson analytical framing: 'targeted provocations highlight the fundamental problem with partial regional ceasefires — they merely redirect rather than resolve hostilities.' **The 'coordinated quietism' framing was a graduated-rhetoric pattern, NOT a structural restraint** — Iran's proxy network can pivot from non-attack (last 7 days) to verbal-blockade within 48 hours. **NEW trigger ADDED at 'watching'**: confirmed missile/drone aimed at Haifa-bound shipping within 14 days = action-tag upgrade to CONFIRMED ACTION; silence through Day 14 = 'rhetoric-only' tag holds. Israel-government and IDF responses to the announcement not surfaced through Tuesday morning IST. Lloyds JWC EastMed AWRP movement will be cleanest operational test — if Lloyds elevates the EastMed war-risk premium, the 'rhetoric-only' tag weakens. **The announcement lands on the same day as Trump's postponement post — re-armed proxy rhetoric overlay on the postponed kinetic axis is a Sadjadpour 'two hubris' second-order signal: Iran's proxy network reasserts deniable optionality precisely when the diplomatic axis pauses.**
sources: Maritime Executive 'Houthis Reboot Red Sea Crisis With 'Blockade' on Port of Haifa'
Atlantic Council / Stimson Center analytical framing
recent_signals.verification: Houthi Haifa announcement = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (Saree statement multi-source; the announcement is a verified public statement). Substantive claim ('blockade operational') = RHETORIC tag (NOT operationalized through confirmed attacks). Houthi Eilat-cessation claim = REPORTED (single-source Saree; independent maritime confirmation of Eilat operational status pending). Mediterranean reach from Yemen = operational reality check (structurally weaker than Red Sea reach).

recent_signals.headline: **A4 RED SEA DAY 7 OF 7 NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW CLOSES UNTRIPPED — May 11 Hodeidah reverts to 'single unclaimed event'** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO recent-incidents page primary, MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement). The trigger 'Second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 Hodeidah-area UKMTO advisory' on strait-of-hormuz node moves watching → untripped_expired. **Houthi non-action during a publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase is the cleanest behavioural divergence from the March-April pattern**: NYT preparation report May 16, Trump 'Calm Before the Storm' May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu phone call May 17, Israeli security cabinet May 17, Saudi drone interception May 17, UAE Barakah drone strike May 17. The houthis→red-sea and red-sea→strait-of-hormuz edges carry forward at high weight but the *absence* of action under a multi-vector US escalation cycle is operationally consistent with Houthi command being instructed by Iran to hold fire. **Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification of Trump posts and Mojtaba Day 11 of state-media silence** — coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise. The investor read: Iran is signalling escalation control, not escalation, against Washington's posturing. The non-action read is structurally validated through one full negative-space cycle.
sources: UKMTO recent-incidents page (operational primary, no new advisory through Monday IST)
Gard Insights 'Red Sea update: Resumption of Houthi campaign' (industry assessment)
recent_signals.verification: Day 7 untripped = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (UKMTO operational primary, MARAD official; multi-day window closes); coordinated quietism reading = CONFIRMED analytical observation (negative-space + Mojtaba silence + TASS/Xinhua restraint all corroborate).

recent_signals.headline: **A8 NEGATIVE-SPACE Day 3 of 7 since May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed explosion — UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' shows NO second incident in southern Red Sea/Hodeidah corridor through May 13-14 morning** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO operational primary). If no second incident by ~May 18 (Day 7), May 11 reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than pattern start. CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61. Houthi non-claim of May 11 remains operative signal. Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92% from Feb-Mar peak (JPost + CNN intel) = Iran-aligned proxy network operating well below historic kinetic ceiling.
sources: Jerusalem Post 'Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92%'
CNN US intel on Iran missile capability
recent_signals.verification: No second Red Sea incident through Day 3 of 7-day window = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (UKMTO primary); CENTCOM redirect tally held = CONFIRMED ABSENCE-OF-CHANGE.

recent_signals.headline: DAY 81 — HOUTHI RED SEA NON-EVENT POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11. UKMTO reported multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km SW of Hodeidah; vessel briefly 'not under command' but continued voyage, no injuries (CONFIRMED ACTION — UKMTO primary, Times of Israel corroborator). **Houthis did NOT claim — attribution UNCONFIRMED.** First Red Sea shipping incident with possible Houthi attribution since Iran's May 4 kinetic restart, breaking the 80-day quiet that had spanned Iran's multi-axis restart + Project Freedom cycles + Hezbollah cross-border reach. The non-claim is itself a signal: deniable Houthi action, action awaiting framing, or unrelated incident. **Trigger window: if second Red Sea incident occurs within 7 days of May 11, the Houthi non-action read collapses.** Until then: single incident, sample size 1, pattern undeclared. Coordination-decay framework not yet falsified but under first material test in 80 days.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO primary multi-source) + UNCONFIRMED attribution (Houthi non-claim; no party has publicly claimed)

recent_signals.headline: DAY 80 RED SEA QUIET — NO HOUTHI ATTACKS ON COMMERCIAL SHIPPING IN PAST 24 HOURS; UNSCR 2812 MONTHLY REPORTING FRAME INTACT (NEXT REPORT JULY 15). UKMTO advisories continue to flag heightened threat level but no incidents. Houthis reiterated rhetorical solidarity with Iran across early-cycle messaging but operational silence is now in 80th consecutive day. Houthi non-response now spans (a) Iran's May 4 multi-axis kinetic restart, (b) US Project Freedom + pause cycle, (c) the May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks, (d) the May 7-8 second day of US tanker strikes, (e) the May 8 Hezbollah Mt. Meron drone reach (corrected to adjacent territory), AND now (f) the Sunday May 10 bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike. The full proxy-restraint pattern is multi-event confirmed: even when other Iranian proxies (Hezbollah) demonstrate first cross-border reach AND when an unclaimed strike occurs near a fellow GCC mediator's coast, Houthis remain quiet. The non-action remains the signal.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED NON-ACTION (intelligence in the absence) — UKMTO + MARAD + Lloyd's List multi-source

recent_signals.headline: DAY 73-74 RED SEA QUIET — HOUTHI NON-RESPONSE TO IRAN MAY 4 MULTI-AXIS KINETIC RESTART INCREASINGLY ANOMALOUS; COORDINATION-DECAY THESIS STRENGTHENS
DAY 65 RED SEA QUIET — IRAN'S HORMUZ-FIRST PROPOSAL VIA PAKISTAN + IRGC 'MORE SEVERE DAMAGES' RHETORIC + LEBANON KINETIC
DAY 64 RED SEA QUIET — UKMTO UPDATE 034 EXPLICITLY DOCUMENTS HOUTHI RHETORIC-WITHOUT-OPERATIONAL-INDICATORS

historical_summaries.summary: 10 signals. Abdul Malik al-Houthi declared group 'prepared to enter the war' with 'fingers on the trigger' in recorded address | Mohammed al-Bukhaiti (political bureau member, not military commander) told NYT: 'the expansion of the conflict to include other countries, | Three weeks into war, no Houthi attacks

22 signals. Day 33: STILL zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | CONFIRMED ABSENCE: Day 34 of war — ZERO confirmed attacks on merchant shipping in Red Sea or Bab el-Mandeb | Day 35: ZERO confirmed Red Sea shipping attacks
